item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy fuck, this is incredibly well-written and feels so realistic that I literally had to look up which parts were real and which weren't. Honestly, now I want to write more Cold-War era SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With myself being a Vietnamese and have already acknowledged of our past sufferings from what being addressed the Vietnam War, the idea that Operation RANCH HAND is an effort to cover up another operation utilizing memetic and (sort of) biological weapon strikes my heart and mind so hard. It's painful, relatable and very well written. Overall, a nice reminder of what we've been through that we shall never at all forget.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic. I love the history of this thing, and how it was developed and refined over time.

I wonder where the Spanish got it from.

item.title: Re: Spoke with a Ghost
item.content:encoded: Hey! Check out this wonderful fanart by the amazing SunnyClockwork!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey, you done posted it.

As I said in chat there is a lot to like about the piece:

  • The connection to Coldest War, with the US using anomalies in its fight in Vietnam, is well explored, and terrifying.
  • The use of Lambda-12 is very well done, with each MTF member having a distinguishable personality.
  • The MTF coming across as rather competent in the face of the unknown as opposed to getting their teeth kicked in as is often the case in recovery/exploration logs is quite refreshing.
  • The history of the anomaly is both intriguing, and provides a lot of springboards for additional writing in this territory, especially with regards to the DoD's occult branch (also Pentagram is a fantastic name and you should feel good about it).

If I had any one nitpick, its that the article as a whole seems to cut out a little too early. As a reader, seeing the interplay between the SCPF and members of the US Government with regards to the SCPF catching them red handed engaging in occult warfare would have been a nice kicker.

Still, its an enjoyable article, and I look forward to seeing its connection to 3rd Law.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. I love the historical background and world-building, especially the contrast between its use in the Spanish-American War and Vietnam. +1

item.title: Spoke with a Ghost
item.content:encoded:
This article has been a couple months in the making. Ironically the only thing that took me two months to write was the exploration log.

This is my first entry into the Coldest War canon. I also plan to include it in the Third Law canon and make it the first second-ever cross-canon article.

Special thanks to Roget, minmin, Chubert, Vezaz, and The Great Hippo for help with brainstorming, streamlining, editing, and revising.

Thanks to Doctor Cimmerian, Modern_Erasmus, Jacob Conwell, and DrBleep for their feedback.

See more of my work on my author page!

Licensing Info:

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/ce/Mosquito_back.jpg - One night in Tayrona National Natural Park resulted in a mosquito-bitten back even though it was protected by t-shirt, bed sheet and hammock. Own work by Servasbemal. This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Unported license.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ranch_Hand_defoiliation_effects_on_Vegetation_in_Vietnam.jpg - A Vietcong trench revealed by U.S. Air Force "Ranch Hand" spraying. It normally took about three days for the spray to start affecting the vegetation. National Museum of the U.S. Air Force photo 071002-F-1234P-021. This image or file is a work of a U.S. Air Force Airman or employee, taken or made as part of that person's official duties. As a work of the U.S. federal government, the image or file is in the public domain in the United States.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%27Ranch_Hand%27_run.jpg - Defoliant spray run, part of Operation Ranch Hand, during the Vietnam War by UC-123B Provider aircraft. National Museum of the U.S. Air Force photo 071002-F-1234P-022. This image or file is a work of a U.S. Air Force Airman or employee, taken or made as part of that person's official duties. As a work of the U.S. federal government, the image or file is in the public domain in the United States.
